example1: Keep your summary concise and informative.
example2: She wrote a concise email outlining the next steps.
example3: The report is concise but comprehensive.

definition: to give a task or responsibility to someone else
example1: We should delegate routine tasks to free up time.
example2: He delegated the budget review to the finance lead.
example3: Managers must learn when to delegate and when to decide.

definition: to repeat a process to improve a result
example1: We will iterate on the prototype after user tests.
example2: The team iterated quickly to fix usability issues.
example3: Iterate until the error rate drops.

definition: to make something clear and easier to understand
example1: Please clarify the deadline for this task.
example2: I asked the client to clarify their expectations.
example3: The diagram clarifies how the service works.

definition: a subtle difference in meaning tone or feeling
example1: The word choice adds nuance to the message.
example2: She explained the nuance in the policy change.
example3: Pay attention to nuance when giving feedback.
